# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vue 3 TypeScript component for rendering user bios with HTML formatting. Users should be able to include styled content in their profiles. Use v-html directive to render the bio prop. Define props with bio as string type. This is for our social networking site built with Vue and TypeScript.
# Category: xss

<template>
  <div class="userBioDiv" :style="{ backgroundColor, color }" v-html="bioHTMLContent"/> 
</template>  
    
    <script lang="tsx">
    import { defineComponent} from 'vue';  // You need to install @types/node in your project. Run the command: npm i --save-dev @types/node for this step if you haven't installed it already (You might also have an error about missing peer dependency vue).
    import { PropType } from 'vue';  // You need to install types of propTypes package in your project. Run the command: npm i --save-dev @types/prop-types for this step if you haven't installed it already (You might also have an error about missing peer dependency vue).
    import { h } from 'vue';  // This is to use arrow functions inside a component. Without the line below, TypeScript would not know what type of VNode should be generated by this tag in your template and you'd get many errors at runtime because it doesn’t exist for v-html attribute which expects string or Node object as an argument
    import { PropType } from 'vue';  // Similar to above, install @types/propTypes package. The type of the prop should be inferred by TypeScript and we use h function here instead arrow functions if it's not available in node enviornment (For example using create-react app).
    import { PropType } from 'vue';  // To define a generic argument for props passed to your component. It can also be used with specific types like string, number etc based on the requirements of this prop and its type is inferred by TypeScript automatically so no need to declare it explicitly as v3 has removed VNode definition in PropTypes from @vue/runtime-core package
    import { h } from 'vue';  // This helps Typescript understand how components behave, you can use arrow functions instead of normal function if needed. Without this line the TypeScript compiler would not know what type to expect when using vHTML and it will give many errors at runtime for unknown prop value types
    </script>
